title: When Cops Discover a Serial Killer's Truck Full of Bodies

beats.description: Dramatic montage of high-tension quotes from family, detectives, and the suspect sets a grim tone before the timeline resets.
Narrator establishes the initial missing person case (Matthew Miller) and detectives begin interviewing family and associates in Grants, NM.
Investigation expands to two more missing persons. Shawn Lannon is introduced as a person of interest; informant tips and search warrants raise suspicion.
First interrogation where detectives confront Shawn with surveillance footage contradicting his alibi, though he is released due to lack of direct evidence.
Airport security discovers a foul-smelling red truck containing dismembered remains of the three missing individuals and a fourth unidentified victim.
Manhunt intensifies as Shawn is identified fleeing to New Jersey. A fifth victim is discovered during a wellness check, confirming a cross-country killing spree.
Shawn is apprehended in Missouri and delivers a detailed, chilling confession covering the murders, dismemberment, and disposal of all five confirmed victims.
Shawn claims responsibility for 11 additional murders, but investigators find contradictions in his stories and lack physical evidence, casting doubt on his claims.
Context on Shawn's military trauma is provided, followed by his plea deal, 95-year sentence, and the adoption of the surviving children.

_analysis_metadata.transcript_preview: He's been missing for over two weeks. >> That's unlike my daughter. It's unlike my daughter not calling us or texting us three weeks. >> I'm telling you all three of them people, man. >> I'm just scared, bro. >> Step one is to tell me where the bodies are. >> Oh, there is. >> All of them dead. Now, what do you think? >> God, I could imagine anything happened to one person. Never lost three. He told me there could be no god because of what he saw. >> I want to know why and what gave him the right. >> One of the most shocking cases in New Mexico history begins with reports of ...
